entries.title: Jacobian Determinant
entries.content: The determinant of the Jacobian matrix, used in coordinate transformations for multiple integrals.
entries.example: Scaling factor dx dy = r dr dtheta in polar coordinates

entries.title: Jacobian Matrix
entries.content: A matrix of all first-order partial derivatives of a vector-valued function.
entries.example: Linearizing non-linear system around equilibrium point

entries.title: Jensen's Inequality
entries.content: An inequality stating that the secant line of a convex function lies above the graph of the function.
entries.example: Proving arithmetic-geometric mean inequality

entries.title: Joint Probability
entries.content: The probability of two or more events occurring simultaneously.
entries.example: Probability of drawing a card that is both a Red card and an Ace

entries.title: Joint Probability Density
entries.content: A function representing the probability distribution of two or more continuous random variables.
entries.formula: f(x, y) where double integral is 1
entries.example: Calculating probability that temperature is >20 and humidity is >60%

entries.title: Joint Variation
entries.content: A variation where a variable varies directly as the product of two or more other variables.
entries.example: Volume of a cylinder varies jointly with height and radius squared

entries.title: Jordan Canonical Form
entries.content: An upper triangular matrix representing a linear operator, simplifying eigenvalue visualization when not diagonalizable.
entries.example: Representing linear systems in control theory

entries.title: Jordan Curve Theorem
entries.content: A theorem stating that a simple closed curve divides the plane into an interior and exterior region.
entries.example: Any continuous loop drawn on paper separates inside from outside

entries.title: Julia Set
entries.content: A fractal set defined by the behavior of chaotic dynamical systems under iteration of complex functions.
entries.example: Iterating z_(n+1) = z_n^2 + c

entries.title: Jump Discontinuity
entries.content: A point where a function has distinct, finite left-hand and right-hand limits.
entries.formula: lim x->c- f(x) != lim x->c+ f(x)
entries.example: The step function y = floor(x) at integer points

entries.title: Justification in Proofs
entries.content: Providing established theorems, axioms, or definitions to validate each step in a mathematical proof.
entries.example: Applying distributive law to simplify expression in proof

entries.title: Juxtaposition in Algebra
entries.content: Indicating multiplication by placing variables or numbers next to each other without an operator.
entries.example: Writing 2x to mean 2 times x

entries.title: J-invariant
entries.content: A modular function of a complex variable that classifies elliptic curves up to isomorphism.
entries.example: Classifying elliptic curves in number theory

entries.title: Jacobi Identity
entries.content: A property of binary operations that determines the relation in Lie algebras.
entries.example: Cross product of vectors satisfies the Jacobi identity

entries.title: Jacobi Method
entries.content: An iterative algorithm for determining the solutions of a strictly diagonally dominant system of linear equations.
entries.example: Approximating large sparse linear systems

entries.title: Jacobi Symbol
entries.content: A generalization of the Legendre symbol to odd composite bottom numbers.
entries.example: Used in primality testing algorithms like Solovay-Strassen

entries.title: Jeffreys Prior
entries.content: A non-informative prior distribution in Bayesian statistics, invariant under reparameterization.
entries.example: Using Jeffreys prior for coin flip probability estimation

entries.title: Joint Cumulative Distribution
entries.content: A function expressing the probability that multiple random variables fall below specified values.
entries.formula: F(x, y) = P(X <= x, Y <= y)
entries.example: Evaluating likelihood that height <= 170cm and weight <= 70kg

entries.title: Joint Entropy
entries.content: A measure of the uncertainty associated with a set of variables.
entries.example: Entropy of two combined data channels

entries.title: Joint Expectation
entries.content: The expected value of a function of two or more random variables.
entries.example: Calculating covariance between variables

entries.title: Jordan Algebra
entries.content: A non-associative algebra over a field whose multiplication satisfies Jordan identity.
entries.example: Formulating quantum mechanics observables

entries.title: Jordan Matrix
entries.content: A matrix block-composed of Jordan blocks.
entries.example: Solving linear differential equations systems

entries.title: Jordan Block
entries.content: A square matrix whose diagonal entries are equal, and whose superdiagonal entries are 1.
entries.example: Basic building block of Jordan canonical form

entries.title: Julia Set Fractal
entries.content: The visualization of the boundary points of the Julia set in the complex plane.
entries.example: Rendering beautiful fractal images of complex functions

entries.title: Joint Distribution Function
entries.content: Function describing probability behavior of multiple random variables combined.
entries.example: Modeling height and weight correlation in health data

entries.title: Jaccard Index
entries.content: A statistic used for gauging the similarity and diversity of sample sets.
entries.formula: J(A, B) = |A intersect B| / |A union B|
entries.example: Comparing words overlap between two documents

entries.title: Jarque-Bera Test
entries.content: A goodness-of-fit test of whether sample data have the skewness and kurtosis matching a normal distribution.
entries.example: Testing if financial returns follow a normal distribution

entries.title: Jonckheere's Trend Test
entries.content: A statistical test for comparing ordered alternative hypotheses among independent samples.
entries.example: Testing if dosage increase monotonically increases response

entries.title: Johnson's Su Distribution
entries.content: A four-parameter family of search probability distributions covering a wide range of skewness and kurtosis.
entries.example: Modeling asset price distribution in risk management

entries.title: Joint Moment Generating Function
entries.content: A function used to generate joint moments of multiple random variables.
entries.example: Finding covariance of variables using derivatives

entries.title: Jacobi Polynomials
entries.content: A class of classical orthogonal polynomials defined on the interval [-1, 1].
entries.example: Solving differential equations in physics

entries.title: Jordan Decomposition
entries.content: Decomposing a matrix or operator into diagonalizable and nilpotent parts.
entries.formula: A = D + N where DN = ND
entries.example: Analyzing matrix exponentials

entries.title: Jordan Canonical Form Matrix
entries.content: The exact matrix configuration representing a Jordan form.
entries.example: Linear system decoupling

entries.title: Julia Fractal
entries.content: Fractal patterns representing Julia sets.
entries.example: Generating patterns on complex plane

entries.title: Joint Probability Mass Function
entries.content: A function giving the probability that discrete variables X and Y equal x and y.
entries.formula: p(x, y) = P(X = x, Y = y)
entries.example: Probability table for roll of two dice

entries.title: Jordan Normal Form
entries.content: Another term for Jordan canonical form.
entries.example: Analyzing matrix powers and limits

entries.title: Jaccard Similarity
entries.content: The Jaccard index value representing similarity percentage.
entries.example: Evaluating recommendation system accuracy

entries.title: Jacobi Elliptic Functions
entries.content: Standard elliptic functions of interest in math analysis, originating from elliptic integrals.
entries.formula: sn(u, k), cn(u, k), dn(u, k)
entries.example: Modeling the motion of an arbitrary pendulum

entries.title: J-homomorphism
entries.content: A mapping from the homotopy groups of orthogonal groups to homotopy groups of spheres.
entries.example: Calculations in algebraic topology

entries.title: Joint Probability Density Function
entries.content: The PDF of two combined continuous random variables.
entries.example: Probability density of wind speed and direction
